root_text.iti94:2.1: “Tathā tathā, bhikkhave, bhikkhu upaparikkheyya yathā yathāssa upaparikkhato bahiddhā cassa viññāṇaṁ avikkhittaṁ avisaṭaṁ ajjhattaṁ asaṇṭhitaṁ anupādāya na paritasseyya.
root_text.iti94:2.2: Bahiddhā, bhikkhave, viññāṇe avikkhitte avisaṭe sati ajjhattaṁ asaṇṭhite anupādāya aparitassato āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇadukkhasamudayasambhavo na hotī”ti.
root_text.iti94:4.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti94:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti94:2.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant should examine in any such a way that their consciousness is neither scattered and diffused externally nor stuck internally, and they are not anxious because of grasping.
translation_text.iti94:2.2: When this is the case and they are no longer anxious, there is for them no coming to be of the origin of suffering—of rebirth, old age, and death in the future.”
translation_text.iti94:3.1: “For one who has given up seven chains,
translation_text.iti94:3.2: a mendicant who has cut the cord,
translation_text.iti94:3.4: there’ll be no more no more future lives for them.”
translation_text.iti94:4.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
